core_mechanism: transient relaxation of a linear dispersive wave field toward a stationary state following a sudden (quench-like) change in initial or boundary conditions, governed by the system's time-dependent propagator (the temporal analog of Fresnel-diffraction response to a sharp cutoff)
brief_reason: The abstract defines quantum transients as temporary features of matter waves before reaching a stationary regime that arise from an unstable initial state or a sudden change in interaction/boundary conditions, with phenomena like diffraction in time and forerunners being the hallmark propagator response of a dispersive field to an abrupt switch.
